title: Privacy and Security: What to Know Before Sharing Family Information
summary: Sharing sensitive family information digitally is common now, but still deserves a moment of real scrutiny before diving in.
subcategory: Privacy, Estate Support, and Getting Started
journeyStage: Privacy, Estate Support, and Getting Started
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Privacy and Security: What to Know Before Sharing Family Information | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: Sharing sensitive family information digitally is common now, but still deserves a moment of real scrutiny before diving in.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Encryption and access controls are baseline expectations, not bonus features.
Knowing what happens to data if you stop using a service matters as much as what happens while you use it.
A platform should make it easy to delete your data, not just easy to add it.

paragraphs: At minimum, any platform handling health, financial, or family coordination information should use encryption and let you control who specifically can access what — these are baseline expectations now, not premium extras.

It's worth asking, before joining anything, what happens to your data if you later stop using the service — whether it's deleted, how quickly, and whether you can request it be removed entirely, not just deactivated.

nextSteps: Confirm encryption and access control basics before sharing sensitive information anywhere.
Ask specifically about data deletion policy.
Don't assume "downloaded the app" means "read the privacy practices.".
disclaimer: General guidance applicable to evaluating any platform, not specific claims about a particular product's current practices.
